feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Supply Chain Management
feed.subtitle: SAP Supply Chain Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Looking to understand support challenges in SAP environments
entry.content:
I’m searching for support challenges in SAP environments

Interested in hearing real examples from support projects, warehouse operations, manufacturing integrations, or AMS environments.

entry.title: Multi-Stage Advanced intercompany process with External Suppliers
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We would appreciate expert input from the SAP community on a design challenge we are currently evaluating in our S/4HANA landscape.
We are evaluating SAP S/4HANA Multi-Level Advanced Intercompany (MLAIC) to support a multi-stage intercompany (buy-sell) chain in a turntable setup (multiple company codes with valuated SIT and end-to-end document flow).

While MLAIC works well for internal intercompany scenarios, we are facing design challenges when integrating an external supplier (e.g., CMO) into the process chain.

⚠️Key Design Gaps / Challenges

  • No standard integration pattern for external supplier involvement
    • Missing linkage between MLAIC document chain and external procurement processes
  • Goods movement & ownership handling
    • Unclear orchestration of GI/GR postings and ownership transfer (valuated SIT vs. externally triggered shipment events). In other words, how can we do an automatic Goods receipt and Goods Issue at Intermediate company when the delivering company has physically dispatched the goods to the receiving company.
  • Tax / Localization complexity
    • Dynamic determination of tax setup in external supplier → intercompany chain scenarios, when the selling company has VAT registration in multiple EU countries and based on the supplier's location, the tax should be determined.

❓Questions to the Community

  • Has anyone implemented MLAIC with external supplier scenarios?
  • What design approaches are used to connect external procurement flows with MLAIC orchestration?
  • How are valuated SIT, GI/GR timing, and ownership transfer handled in such hybrid setups?
  • Any best practices for managing complex VAT/tax determination in these flows?

entry.title: Adding Custom Field in LTMC Template
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to use a UII field instead of the standard Serial Number field during inventory migration using LTMC (Material Inventory Balance object).

Could you please help with:

  • Whether it is possible to add a custom UII field into the LTMC template
  • The process involved in creating and mapping the field
  • Whether this requires LTMOM enhancement, append structures, or BAPI enhancement

Any guidance or standard approach for this requirement would be helpful.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Combined Multiple purchase Requisitions into single PR post MRP Run
entry.content:
Dear Team, 

I have activated Collective requirement in MRP4 view of master data where the requirement date is same for single Material with multiple PR.  As per requirement, the system should generate single PR POST MRP Run. Along with that lot size procedure is taken  MB in MRP 1 view. Kindly suggest how this can it be possible to clubbed the requirement into single PR.

entry.title: WM Inbound Putaway Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi, I would like to clarify a scenario in inbound putaway in public cloud:

I have defined a pallet with a capacity of 1000 kg, and I have enabled the setting “Do Not Split Putaway” during the putaway process.

However, in a situation where 3 pallets arrive at the same time, I would like to understand:

  1. How does the system handle multiple pallets when “Do Not Split Putaway” is active?
  2. What is the best way to handle this scenario properly — should we use Handling Unit (HU) Management so that each pallet is processed individually?
  3. Are there recommended configurations (e.g., HU type, storage type settings, capacity check) to ensure each pallet is put away correctly without violating the “Do Not Split Putaway” rule?

Appreciate your guidance on best practice for this setup. Thank you.

entry.title: PartnerEdge Build: SAP Transportation Management (TM) Not Appearing in Price List
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are an ISV registered under the SAP PartnerEdge Build program and are working to build a certified integration between our platform and SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) embedded in S/4HANA.

Our product is a freight market intelligence and data platform — we deliver real-time spot rates, capacity indices, and carrier benchmarking data via API. The use case for SAP TM users is surfacing this market data natively within transportation planning and carrier selection workflows.

The problem: We cannot find the SAP TM partner test/dev environment anywhere in our PartnerEdge price list.

Here is the navigation path we have exhausted:
• Categories → Cloud - S/4HANA Applications → Line of Business Portfolio → Cloud - S/4HANA Digital Supply Chain
• The only three products listed there are SAP Project and Resource Management (8021070), SAP Service and Asset Manager (8005710), and SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation (8016066) — none of which are relevant to Transportation Management.

We have also checked:
• Cloud - S/4HANA Digital Supply Chain (top-level shortcut) — same three products
• Cloud - S/4HANA Digital Enterprise Platform
• Partner Test, Demo, and Development On-Premise

We have already contacted the SAP Partner Portal support team, who were unable to resolve this and redirected us here.

Our specific questions:
1. What is the correct product SKU / license for an SAP PartnerEdge Build partner to access an SAP TM development and test environment on S/4HANA Cloud?
2. Does SAP TM require a separate entitlement request outside the standard price list — i.e., does it need to be manually enabled by SAP's partner team?
3. Is SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) a prerequisite that needs to be provisioned first before TM becomes visible?
4. Has anyone else run into this and found a resolution path?

Any guidance from partners who have successfully navigated this, or from SAP product/partner team members, would be greatly appreciated. Happy to share more detail about our integration use case if helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: PartnerEdge Build: SAP Transportation Management (TM) Not Appearing in Price List
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are an ISV registered under the SAP PartnerEdge Build program and are working to build a certified integration between our platform and SAP Transportation Management (SAP TM) embedded in S/4HANA.

Our product is a freight market intelligence and data platform; we deliver real-time spot rates, capacity indices, and carrier benchmarking data via API. The use case for SAP TM users is surfacing this market data natively within transportation planning and carrier selection workflows.

The problem: We cannot find the SAP TM partner test/dev environment anywhere in our PartnerEdge price list.

Here is the navigation path we have exhausted:
• Categories → Cloud - S/4HANA Applications → Line of Business Portfolio → Cloud - S/4HANA Digital Supply Chain
• The only three products listed there are SAP Project and Resource Management (8021070), SAP Service and Asset Manager (8005710), and SAP Intelligent Product Recommendation (8016066); none of which are relevant to Transportation Management.

We have also checked:
• Cloud - S/4HANA Digital Supply Chain (top-level shortcut); same three products
• Cloud - S/4HANA Digital Enterprise Platform
• Partner Test, Demo, and Development On-Premise

We have already contacted the SAP Partner Portal support team, who were unable to resolve this and redirected us here.

Our specific questions:
1. What is the correct product SKU / license for an SAP PartnerEdge Build partner to access an SAP TM development and test environment on S/4HANA Cloud?
2. Does SAP TM require a separate entitlement request outside the standard price list ( i.e., does it need to be manually enabled by SAP's partner team?)
3. Is SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) a prerequisite that needs to be provisioned first before TM becomes visible?
4. Has anyone else run into this and found a resolution path?

Any guidance from partners who have successfully navigated this, or from SAP product/partner team members, would be greatly appreciated. Happy to share more detail about our integration use case if helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: AATP using SBC with PAC using supply creation doesn't create temporary supplies
entry.content:
Hello experts, 

we are using S/4HANA private cloud 2025 with SP/ FP stack 01 (02/2026) FPS. Furthermore, we are using CDP in PP/DS. I tried to set up SBC with PAC using supply creation, but I don't get temporary supplies. 

I wanted to try SBC with PAC using supply creation for a simple MTO process using CDP. I'm aware that on level of the PAC check characteristics are not supported. But during supply creation on component level CDP is supported, right?

The set up: 


We are using availability check group Z1 for PAC. I copied from 01. Z2 is for PPAC which works perfectly fine for the same master data by the way. 

master data:

 

Availability check result: 
no supply creation

As mentioned before, for trying availabiltiy check type Z2 with PPAC works perfectly fine. Did I miss something?

What I wanted to try was a BOP simulation run after sales order creation since it was mentioned in some blocks that this is supported. I set it up and tried it but I then I saw that no temporary supplies were created which must have happened already before I tried BOP. 

Furthermore I found the learning journey: https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-advanced-production-planning-with-sap-s-4hana-pp-ds/exploring-supply-creation-based-confirmation-sbc--1

Under PPAC it is stated that the availability check results can be displayed in BOP monitor App. I guess this is only supported for SBC using PAC instead of PPAC, right?

 

BR

entry.title: EWM Product has Block Profile E2 Warning but it is stopping the Physical Counting on RF
entry.content: EWM Product has Block Profile E2 Warning but it is stopping the Physical Counting on RF .

entry.title: Supply chain management
entry.content:
Hi Fellows

I hope you all are doing well,

I worked in supply chain company, I have worked as customer services (accounts receivable, accounts payable, inventory management) then I worked as warehouse operator (pick and pack and deliver orders) what should i do in SAP to enhance my skills

Thanks

Gurmukh singh

entry.title: In Which Scenarios Is POSC Truly Necessary?
entry.content:
If RF is already recording every movement, users are already making decisions, and Internal WPTs are controlling warehouse movements, then in what situations does POSC provide additional value?

I am trying to understand where experienced consultants see the biggest benefit of POSC beyond visibility and movement control.

entry.title: n Which Scenarios Is POSC Truly Necessary?
entry.content:
If RF is already recording every movement, users are already making decisions, and Internal WPTs are controlling warehouse movements, then in what situations does POSC provide additional value?

I am trying to understand where experienced consultants see the biggest benefit of POSC beyond visibility and movement control

entry.title: SAP EWM Automatic Warehouse Task creation Fails for Same line item contains HU and Loose material.
entry.content:
Title: Automatic WT Creation Issue for Mixed HU and Loose Stock from Same Inbound Delivery Item in SAP EWM

Hello Experts,

I am facing an issue with automatic Warehouse Task (WT) creation during inbound putaway in SAP EWM.

Scenario

  • Automatic WT creation is configured and working successfully.

  • A single Warehouse Process Type (WPT) is used for both HU-managed and loose stock.

  • Storage Type Search determines different destination storage types:

    • HU stock → HD Rack Storage Type

    • Loose stock → HL Rack Storage Type

  • Quantity Offsetting is configured to differentiate packed and loose quantities.

Expected Behavior

For an inbound delivery item containing both:

  • Packed quantity in an HU

  • Loose quantity outside the HU

the system should create:

> One WT for the HU stock to the HD Rack.

> One WT for the loose stock to the HL Rack.

Actual Behavior

When the inbound delivery item contains both HU and loose stock:

  • Automatic WT is created only for the HU Warehouse Request item.

  • No automatic WT is created for the loose Warehouse Request item.

  • The loose stock remains in the GR area and requires manual WT creation.

Additional Findings

  • If only loose stock is received, automatic WT creation works correctly.

  • If only HU stock is received, automatic WT creation works correctly.

  • The issue occurs only when HU and loose stock are received for the same inbound delivery item.

  • Manual WT creation for the loose stock works successfully and determines the correct destination storage type and bin.

  • SLG1 logs show PPF action evaluation messages, but no clear error indicating why WT creation is skipped for the loose Warehouse Request.

Question

Has anyone experienced a similar issue where automatic WT creation processes only the HU Warehouse Request item and skips the loose Warehouse Request item created from the same inbound delivery item?

Are there any known settings, PPF conditions, BAdIs, quantity offsetting limitations, or SAP Notes that should be checked in this scenario?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: User Defined custom categories in aATP Scope of Check
entry.content:
We have a requirement to exclude a few requirements / receipts in the scope of check based on certain conditions (like item category or schedule line category in a sales order or delivery type from the delivery documents). In gATP, it was very easy for us to just flip the ATP categories in APO and exclude them in the scope of check.

However, there is no easy option in aATP to achieve the same. Want to know from the community if anyone has encountered such requirement and have achieved the same in aATP?

Thanks,

Regards, Guru

entry.title: Tips for a Trainee in IBP Portifolio Management
entry.content:
Hello, this is the first time I am using this platform.
It's been almost 3 months I was hired to work as IBP consultant and for a couple of weeks and on I have been trying to assist a IBP Portifolio Manager at my company. 

This small shift caused me to change my goals and now I am lost. 

Would you guys tell me some tips, maybe a roadmap, things that I need to do,  both soft and hard skills, to excel at this challenge?

Now, I am reading stuff about SAP Activate and participating at MS Teams meetings, and it seems not enough preparation for that job to me. 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Duplicate Serial Number while doing OBD - PGI
entry.content: Done PGI with Material 1 and serial number 1, and again with material 1 and serial number 1 entering it was not restricted and allowing to do PGI. It has to be restricted, could you give us a suggestion.

entry.title: Query regarding the navigation error during creation of PO in "Process Purchase Requisitions" app.
entry.content:
Hello All,

Is there anyone encounter the error when converting purchase requisition to purchase order using "Process Purchase Requisition" application? The error prompt and saying that "Navigation to this application is not supported".

What is the possible cause of this, and can anyone give me solution?

Thank you!

entry.title: How to automate POF1 creation after POP1
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are using SAP S/4HANA and would like to automate the creation of POF1 (Packing Instruction Determination) records whenever a POP1 (Packing Instruction) is created.

Is there any standard SAP functionality, BAdI, user exit, enhancement spot, API, BAPI, or function module available to achieve this? If not, what is the recommended enhancement approach in S/4HANA? 

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Icon
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone tell me what this icon is for?

We have updated SAP version and can no longer find this in the new one.

It is found in Create TO from TR section.

Thanks

entry.title: Industry Benchmarking to Implement SAP Ecosystem
entry.content:
One of the proven way to implementation SAP System in an enterprise and implement a Digital Transformation Strategy- follow the steps below:

  1. Focus on the current business process and clarify its flow.
  2.  Then Map the current business process with APQC process codes. (PCF- Global Framework)
  3. Follow OSB for Industry-Specific Process Benchmarking and develop Standards.
  4. Align the SAP Ecosystem as per Business Process Re-engineering

For more details, please comment your queries.

Follow the link to read more posts. https://www.linkedin.com/in/abhishek-khare-93a40237/
